pattern: Counting subarrays whose sum lands inside a range becomes a counting-inversions problem on prefix sums: for each prefix you count how many earlier prefixes sit within a sliding window, which a merge sort or balanced order-statistic structure does in n log n.
hints: Rewrite the sum of a subarray as the difference of two prefix sums, so the question turns into pairs of prefixes whose difference falls in a fixed band.
For a right prefix value p, the valid left prefixes are those in the interval from p minus upper to p minus lower; you need to count earlier prefixes inside a moving window.
Sort the prefix sums with a merge sort and, while merging, for each left-half value count how many right-half values fall in its band; accumulate those counts to get the answer in n log n time.

edge_inputs.description: mixed signs with a small symmetric range
single element equal to the only allowed sum
all positive, range that includes several windows
lower equals upper so only exact-sum subarrays count
large magnitudes whose prefix differences need wide integers
single negative element inside the range

stress.description: random arrays of small integers with a moderate range so many subarrays qualify
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(-20, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    lo = rng.randint(-30, 0)
    hi = rng.randint(0, 30)
    return [nums, lo, hi]
stress.note: moderate range keeps the answer large and stresses the windowed count

stress.description: random arrays with large magnitudes to stress integer width
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000000000, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    lo = rng.randint(-100000, 0)
    hi = rng.randint(0, 100000)
    return [nums, lo, hi]
